version.approval_notes:
OpenPath Firefox extension review notes

Purpose
- Browser companion for managed OpenPath devices in education.
- Explains blocked pages/resources, supports access requests when enabled, checks local allowlist state through the OpenPath native host, and blocks known Google game surfaces.

Permission rationale
- <all_urls>: evaluates managed navigation/resource requests and applies path, subdomain, Google game, and local Windows dependency-overlay policy across web origins.
- webRequest/webRequestBlocking: detect and apply managed blocked navigation/resource decisions. On Windows, eligible subresource requests are reduced to anchorHost, dependencyHost, and requestType before local native-host validation.
- webNavigation: tracks the top-level anchor host per tab and clears per-tab state on navigation.
- tabs: scopes badge and popup data to the active tab, and redirects already-open tabs to the blocked-page screen when local policy stops allowing their host.
- nativeMessaging: talks only to the local OpenPath native host on the same computer.
- storage: keeps managed config and local runtime state in browser storage.
- clipboardWrite: copies blocked-domain lists only after a user action.

Network/data behavior
- Manifest data collection declares required browsingActivity: managed unblock requests transmit blocked-domain activity and related request context to the configured OpenPath service.
- User-initiated blocked-page and popup access requests verify the install-time browsingActivity permission before sending request details to the OpenPath service.
- Google game blocking is local browser policy via webRequest for known Snake, doodle-game, and logo-game surfaces. A Google Search/Doodles visual guard content script locally neutralizes detected game widgets, sending only a best-effort local diagnostic to the background runtime.
- Content scripts: an isolated-world page activity relay, a MAIN-world page-resource observer for local diagnostics, and the Google visual guard. The background runtime makes no remote requests from page-resource candidates.
- Windows runtime dependency handling is local-only: the runtime sends only anchorHost, dependencyHost, and requestType to the native host, which validates locally and writes exact-host Acrylic overlays. Nothing is sent to the OpenPath service or synced remotely.
- Open-tab reconciliation is fully local: Firefox polls a get-policy-version stamp (whitelist mtime:size; no contents, no network) and redirects newly-disallowed open tabs to the local blocked-page screen, transmitting nothing.
- Firefox Core does not include Android support, remote automatic AJAX/page-resource allowlisting, or live/automatic AMO upload.
- No third-party analytics, profiling, advertising, or sale/sharing of browser data is included.

Source review
- Human-readable source is attached; see SOURCE_REVIEW_NOTES.md in that archive for rebuild and review notes.

release_notes.en-US:
OpenPath Firefox companion build for managed education devices.

This version includes the blocked-page/request flow, local OpenPath native-host integration, path/subdomain enforcement, local Google games enforcement, Google Search/Doodles visual guard content script, page activity relay, and Windows local exact-host dependency overlays without remote automatic allowlisting or automatic upload. It does not include third-party analytics or telemetry.
